title: Connection Reuse, Pools, HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and Concurrency · Browser and Node Client Systems · Contract Lens: Http2 Multiplexing
summary: A Node rendering service and browsers use Fetch over different pools and protocol negotiation while concurrency and stream backpressure remain bounded. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts http2 multiplexing increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch connection lifecycle and concurrency plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model HTTP/2 streams, field compression, flow control, concurrency limits, and connection-level failure. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Explain persistent HTTP/1.1 connections, body completion, idle reuse, pipelining limits, and close behavior. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Predict Http2 Multiplexing · Theory Http2 Multiplexing
steps.instruction:
theory case — TypeScript Fetch evidence model schedules 32 operations across HTTP/1.1 keep-alive, an HTTP/2 connection limited to 4 streams, and optional HTTP/3. Bound queues and memory, reuse clients, finish bodies, and distinguish transport from semantics. Competency probe: Model HTTP/2 streams, field compression, flow control, concurrency limits, and connection-level failure. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
